title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Gwendolyn and Jules interview Mr. Calbreath to gather information regarding a woman named Helen. Mr. Calbreath describes her as appearing anxious and mentions a house called St. Catherine's Villa, which was once rented by a soldier and later auctioned to the Elwes family, a group of sisters known for being strongly anti-Catholic. Following this lead, Jules requests marriage records from the Kennington Registry Office and discovers a marriage certificate from Friday, August 7th, between Kellvin James Harrydi and Helen Spenceley Kennedy. This discovery puzzles the pair, as they previously believed Helen had divorced and moved away.

While walking along the seafront in Deal, Gwendolyn encounters Miss Marple, who is visiting the seaside for health reasons. Gwendolyn informs Miss Marple about their investigation and the newspaper advertisements they placed seeking information on Helen Spendlove-Haliday, née Kennedy. Miss Marple observes Gwendolyn’s underlying anxiety regarding the case.

Later, Jules and Gwendolyn receive a response to their advertisement from Dr. James Kennedy of Highcliffe Manor in Woodley Porton, who identifies himself as Helen Spengler Kennedy's brother and requests information regarding her whereabouts. Jules and Gwendolyn travel to Woodrailton to meet him. At Highcliffe Manor, they encounter a stern, dark-skinned woman before being received by the elderly, piercing-eyed Dr. Kennedy. Jules presents a rehearsed story, claiming they are searching for Gwendolyn's childhood acquaintances. Initially cold and hostile, Dr. Kennedy's demeanor shifts instantly when Gwendolyn identifies herself as the daughter of his sister's second husband, using her maiden name, Hardy. Dr. Kennedy recognizes her, asking if she is "Gwenith."
